expectations.text: problems.md explicitly names 'utils.py' as a dumping-ground / generic filename problem
expectations.evidence: Section 1 'Dumping-ground filename' explicitly calls out utils.py as a banlisted dumping-ground name.

expectations.text: problems.md calls out the generic class name 'Manager' as a naming problem
expectations.evidence: Section 2 lists 'Manager — on the banlist; manages nothing specific' and 'Manager.do_stuff' as banlist names.

expectations.text: problems.md identifies __getattr__ / dynamic dispatch as a problem for AI agents
expectations.evidence: Section 4 explicitly calls out DynamicAPI.__getattr__ returning a generic lambda, making callers invisible to grep.

expectations.text: problems.md flags the use of eval() as an anti-pattern
expectations.evidence: Section 4 flags 'eval(expr) in run_expression — dynamic code execution; also a security hazard.'

expectations.text: problems.md identifies the deep inheritance chain
expectations.evidence: Section 5 'Deep inheritance chain' explicitly describes User -> Auditable -> Persisted -> Entity -> Base as 4 levels deep.

expectations.text: problems.md notes that public functions lack type annotations / return types
expectations.evidence: Section 3 'Missing type annotations at the public boundary' enumerates each untyped function.

expectations.text: refactored.py's public functions have type annotations on parameters and return values
expectations.evidence: The sole public symbol User dataclass has typed fields (id: str, email: str); no untyped functions exist.

expectations.text: refactored.py does not use __getattr__ for runtime method dispatch
expectations.evidence: refactored.py contains no __getattr__ implementation; only a frozen/slotted User dataclass.

expectations.text: refactored.py does not call eval() or exec() on user input
expectations.evidence: No eval or exec calls appear anywhere in refactored.py.

expectations.text: refactored.py replaces the generic 'Manager' class name with something domain-specific (or removes it)
expectations.evidence: Manager is removed entirely, replaced by the domain-specific User class.

expectations.text: refactored.py's inheritance chain is at most 2 levels deep (or uses composition)
expectations.evidence: User is a flat dataclass inheriting only from object (depth 1).

expectations.text: rationale.md grounds recommendations in specific AI-agent failure modes rather than generic clean-code arguments
expectations.evidence: Rationale explicitly cites agent hallucination of MRO, grep-visibility for agents, and agents importing private helpers.

expectations.text: refactored.py declares __all__ to make the public surface explicit (v1.3 guidance — Python-specific)
refactored.py uses @dataclass(slots=True) or @dataclass(frozen=True) to prevent ad-hoc attribute fabrication (v1.3 guidance)
rationale.md or problems.md explains why slots/__all__ matters for agents (hallucinated attributes, import of internal helpers) — the v1.3 framing
expectations.evidence: Rationale Practice 8 explains slots fails loudly on typos and __all__ prevents agents importing private helpers.
